steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for the most logical transition. The first sentence describes citizens voting for parties. The blank introduces the next step: once votes are counted, seats are awarded proportionally. The relationship is sequential — event 1 happens, then event 2 follows.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Conversely,: Signals a contrast or reversal. There is no contrast between voting and seat distribution — they are steps in the same process. ✗
  • Choice B — In other words,: Signals restatement. The second sentence adds new information (vote counting, seat allocation) rather than rephrasing the first. ✗
  • Choice C — However,: Signals opposition. The two sentences are not in opposition. ✗
  • Choice D — Then,: Signals the next step in a sequence. Citizens first vote, then votes are counted and seats distributed — a clean temporal sequence. ✓
D is correct.
